title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture (every hero is a superhero)
statement_text: Every hero is a superhero, i.e., a tournament $H$ is a superhero if and only if $H$ is a hero.
context_text: A digraph $H$ is a superhero if for every integer $\alpha \geq 1$ there is a number $g(H,\alpha)$ such that $\chi(D) \leq g(H,\alpha)$ for every $H$-free digraph $D$ with $\alpha(D) \leq \alpha$. It was already known that every superhero is a hero; Aboulker, Charbit and Naserasr asked whether the converse holds. The paper's abstract states this answers their question.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full as Theorem 1.3 (every hero is a superhero), thereby completely characterizing all superheroes.
notes: PDF source. Stated as a conjecture/question in the introduction and resolved by the main result of the paper.

title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: For every digraph $H$, there is $\varepsilon > 0$ such that if $D$ is an $H$-free digraph, then $\chi(D) \leq |V(D)|^{1-\varepsilon}$.
context_text: This conjecture (attributed to reference [8] in the paper) generalises the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture to digraphs. It is open even when $H$ is the oriented triangle $C_3$, and can be viewed as a strengthening of the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture. The superhero framework of the paper is motivated by it.
paper_contribution: Not directly proved; mentioned as primary motivation. The paper's results (Theorem 1.3 and Theorem 1.7) are partial steps toward understanding this conjecture in the dense digraph setting.
notes: PDF source. The authorship of reference [8] is not given in the extracted text.

title: Erdős–Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
statement_text: For every tournament $H$, there exists a constant $\varepsilon(H) > 0$ such that every $H$-free tournament $T$ satisfies $\beta(T) \geq \varepsilon(H)|V(T)|$.
context_text: Here $\beta(T)$ denotes the maximum size of an acyclic subset of $V(T)$. The paper presents this as one formulation of the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture and notes that Conjecture 1.1 can be viewed as a strengthening of it. The special case $H = C_3$ would follow from the paper's own Conjecture 1.6 with $\varepsilon = 1/(2\ell)$.
paper_contribution: Mentioned as background; the paper's Conjecture 1.6, if true, would imply the $H = C_3$ special case.
notes: PDF source. Stated in its digraph/acyclicity formulation using $\beta(T)$.

title: Conjecture 1.6
statement_text: There is an integer $\ell$ such that if $D$ is a $C_3$-free digraph with $\alpha(D) \leq \alpha$, then $\chi(D) \leq \alpha^\ell$.
context_text: The authors propose this polynomial bound as a target for the chromatic number of $C_3$-free digraphs with bounded independence number. They note that if true, it would imply the $H = C_3$ special case of the Erdős–Hajnal conjecture with $\varepsilon := 1/(2\ell)$. As partial evidence, they prove the weaker factorial bound $\chi(D) \leq 35^{\alpha-1}\alpha!$ in Theorem 1.7.
notes: PDF source. Introduced with the phrase "we believe that the following statement may be true."
